title: T-005 — Drizzle migration: downtime_events + downtime_source_enum + RLS
prompt:
# T-005 — Drizzle migration: downtime_events table + downtime_source_enum

PRD: 08-PRODUCTION-PRD.md §9.6, §16.4 V-PROD-06/22

## Goal
Add Drizzle migration creating downtime_source_enum and downtime_events per §9.6 with GENERATED ALWAYS AS duration_min STORED column, FK to downtime_categories, RLS.

## Implementation contract
packages/db/schema/production/downtime-events.ts: pgEnum downtime_source_enum ('manual','wo_pause','plc_auto','changeover'). Table downtime_events: PK id UUID, tenant_id, line_id text, wo_id REFERENCES work_orders nullable, category_id REFERENCES downtime_categories (02-SETTINGS), source enum NOT NULL, started_at NOT NULL, ended_at, duration_min INTEGER GENERATED ALWAYS AS (CASE WHEN ended_at IS NOT NULL THEN EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM ended_at - started_at)/60 END) STORED (V-PROD-06), shift_id, operator_id, reason_notes, plc_fault_code, recorded_by, recorded_at default now, ext_jsonb. Indexes idx_downtime_line_time, idx_downtime_category, idx_downtime_open partial WHERE ended_at IS NULL. RLS by tenant_id.

## Files (scope)
- packages/db/schema/production/downtime-events.ts [create]
- packages/db/schema/production/enums.ts [modify]
- packages/db/schema/production/index.ts [modify]
- packages/db/migrations/NNNN_downtime_events.sql [create]
- packages/db/__tests__/downtime-events-schema.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given INSERT with started_at and ended_at, when row queried, then duration_min equals (ended_at-started_at) in minutes, computed by GENERATED column (V-PROD-06 not manually settable)
2. Given source='wo_pause' INSERT with wo_id NULL, when row inserted, then validate-trigger or app-level check is delegated to T-016 — base table allows NULL wo_id with comment noting V-PROD-22 enforced at API
3. Given migration applied, when pg_indexes queried, then idx_downtime_open partial WHERE ended_at IS NULL exists

## Test strategy
- RED: schema test asserting GENERATED ALWAYS AS duration_min STORED + enum values before schema exists
- Run 'pnpm --filter db drizzle:generate && pnpm --filter db test downtime-events-schema'

## Risk red lines
- Do not let users SET duration_min directly (breaks V-PROD-06)
- Do not change enum values without migration plan
- Do not skip RLS

pipeline_inputs.description: downtime_events table with downtime_source_enum, GENERATED ALWAYS duration_min, partial open index, RLS.
pipeline_inputs.details: Per §9.6: pgEnum downtime_source_enum with 4 values. Table has duration_min as GENERATED ALWAYS AS (...) STORED — not user-set (V-PROD-06). Partial index idx_downtime_open WHERE ended_at IS NULL for fast lookups of currently-open events. category_id FK to downtime_categories from 02-SETTINGS. RLS by tenant_id.
out_of_scope: Do not implement WO PAUSE side-effect
Do not seed downtime_categories (02-SETTINGS owns seed)
Do not enforce V-PROD-22 in DB (delegated to API per PRD note)
acceptance_criteria: Given INSERT with both timestamps, when row queried, then duration_min computed by GENERATED column matches minute diff
Given INSERT attempting to SET duration_min, when run, then DB rejects (cannot insert into generated column)
Given migration applied, when pg_indexes queried, then idx_downtime_open partial WHERE ended_at IS NULL exists
test_strategy: RED: schema test asserting GENERATED column + enum values before schema exists
Run 'pnpm --filter db drizzle:generate && pnpm --filter db test downtime-events-schema'
risk_red_lines: Do not let users set duration_min (V-PROD-06)
